title: High General
content:
{{Top|leg}}
The title of '''High General''' indicated a senior position within the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]]. A High General normally controlled a [[Systems Army/Legends|Systems Army]], a largely bureaucratic level of command immediately subordinate to the [[Surface Marshal]] in charge of a [[Sector Army/Legends|Sector Army]], and consisting simply of a non-combatant headquarters detail, responsible for coordinating front-line [[Corps/Legends|Corps]] and [[Army/Legends|Armies]], and keeping track of the resources available within a sector, so that they could be deployed in support of those field formations as required. They were sometimes referred to by the title of Commander.

==Other military forces==
[[File:Rebel_High_General.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] High General]]
The title of High General was not limited to the Imperial military. The title was used as far back as the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]] by [[Ajunta Pall]], the leader of the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]]. Shortly before the outbreak of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the military commander of the planet [[Typha-Dor]] was High General [[Bycha]], and during the subsequent conflict, the rank of [[High Jedi General/Legends|High Jedi General]] was given to the [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi Generals]] commanding the ten [[Systems Army/Legends|Systems Armies]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. These Jedi officers were presumably in some ways prototypes of the later High Generals of the Imperial Army, but the significance of the rank of High General may have evolved considerably under the Empire, as the organization of the associated Systems Army formation certainly did. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the title was also used by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].

==Notable High Generals==
*[[Ajunta Pall]] ([[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]])
*[[Cassio Tagge/Legends|Cassio Tagge]]
*[[Hestiv]]
*[[Sutt Ramic]]
*[[Touno]]
*[[Paltr Carvin]]

==Behind the scenes==
The title of High General appears only in a short paragraph in the ''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'', where it is given to officers set above the [[General/Legends|Generals]] in command of a standard field Army of four [[Corps/Legends|Corps]] formations, but below the [[Surface Marshal]], who is the local [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] or a deputized commander in charge of all Imperial Army operations inside his sector. The authors may have intended there to be two High Generals within a Sector Army, but the text would also allow a single High General to serve as an administrative deputy to the Surface Marshal, or perhaps varying numbers of them according to the strength of forces deployed.

A further unknown is the exact meaning of the title of High General. Within the hierarchy given in the ''Imperial Sourcebook'', a High General is implicitly equivalent to a [[Fleet Admiral/Legends|Fleet Admiral]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]; in spite of the superficial similarity of terminology, the Navy rank of [[High Admiral]] is in contrast equal to [[Surface Marshal]]. But it is not completely clear that any of these positions are proper hierarchical ranks, rather than titles signifying certain levels of senior command and borne additionally to substantive ranks.

Understanding the exact role and status of a High General in the Imperial Army is further hindered by the fact that [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] sources offer few examples of officers explicitly identified as holding this rank. The only one appears to be High General [[Sutt Ramic]], the commander of one of the three [[Golan III Space Defense NovaGun|Golan III]] battle stations protecting [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]] in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], and said to be the senior military officer based at the financial capital of Imperial Space. It is unclear whether Ramic's seniority is simply due to his rank in the hierarchy, or whether it reflects a specific commission, and if the latter, whether his sphere of command was limited to Muunilinst's defenses or its specific star system, or whether it extended further. Additionally, it is unclear whether the command hierarchy at Muunilinst is typical of Imperial Space as a whole, and whether the hierarchy at this time corresponded to the models of twenty years earlier.
